properties.title: Midshipman David Porter Sighting (1823)
properties.description: The first American Skunk Ape sighting on record was reported by David Porter in 1823.
Witnesses reported an unknown ape-like creature drawn to the newly constructed bat tower.
properties.title: Perky Bat Tower Incident (1929)

properties.description: The earliest published report of a Skunk Ape described a man whose car was accosted by one of the creatures.
properties.title: Suwannee County Roadside Encounter (1942)

properties.description: A woman observed what she initially thought was a tall man in a long raincoat.
properties.title: Bardin Booger Sighting (1947)

properties.description: Several reports of the "Bardin Booger."
properties.title: Bardin Booger Sightings (1947)

properties.description: Three hunters reported that a large, smelly, ape-like creature invaded their camp.
properties.title: Big Cypress National Preserve Camp Invasion (1957)

properties.description: Several family members encountered an ape-like creature around their rural home.
properties.title: Alachua County 1st Encounter (1963)

properties.description: A witness reported an unknown biped animal peeking into their home windows at night.
properties.title: Alachua County 2nd Encounter (1963)

properties.description: A witness reported seeing a 5- to 7-foot ape-like creature.
properties.title: Broward County Sighting (1971)

properties.description: An armed search party had assembled to hunt for two large ape-like creatures that had scared some children
properties.title: Broward County Trailer Park Incident (1971)

properties.description: Marvin Lewis and Ernie Milner reported shooting at a tall, ape-like creature.
properties.title: Lantana Shooting Incident (1974)

properties.description: Just after midnight on January 9, 1974, Richard Lee Smith hit something with his car near the Everglades.
properties.title: Pembroke Pines Car Incident (1974)

properties.description: Farmer Buddy Sterrett reported seeing a Skunk Ape lift one of his hogs.
properties.title: Lake Worth Hog Incident (1974)

properties.description: Security guard Cary Kantor reported shooting at a Skunk Ape after it approached him from out of the woods
properties.title: Wellington Security Guard Shooting (1974)

properties.description: Vietnam War veteran and former police officer Charles Stoeckman and his thirteen-year-old son Charlie saw an eight- or nine-foot-tall creature.
properties.title: Key Largo Stoeckman Sighting (1977)

properties.description: Two workers reported seeing a seven-foot-tall hairy ape-like creature sipping water from a lake on the golf course.
properties.title: Delray Beach Golf Course Sighting (1977)

properties.description: A group of tourists on a bus tour reported seeing a seven-foot, red-haired ape running through the Everglades.
properties.title: Everglades Tour Bus Sighting (1997)

properties.description: David Shealy noticed strange tracks around wildlife bait stands laden with lima beans that had been raided.
properties.title: Ochopee Fire Control District Chief's Sighting (1997)

properties.description: Tour operators Steve Goodbread and Dow Rowland, along with their guests, reported sightings.
properties.title: Multiple Skunk Ape Sightings by Many Witnesses (1997)

properties.description: Skunk Ape researcher Dave Shealy claimed to have captured a video of the elusive creature in Big Cypress National Preserve.
properties.title: Dave Shealy's Skunk Ape Video (2000)

properties.description: Diana Sideri-Everdell had a potential Skunk Ape sighting while walking trails in Bird Rookery Swamp near Naples.
properties.title: Bird Rookery Swamp Encounter (2013)

properties.description: Report by Matthew McKamey and a friend.
properties.title: Lettuce Lake Park Canoe Encounter (2015)

properties.description: Emily Jacobs was walking her dog when she spotted a large, hairy creature with dark, matted fur standing nearly seven feet tall.
properties.title: Apopka Wilderness Trail Sighting (2023)

properties.description: Dave Shealy mentioned multiple sightings in this area, suggesting it may harbor more than one Skunk Ape.
properties.title: Kissimmee Marshlands Sighting (2024)

properties.description: Multiple reports
properties.title: Interstate 4 and Interstate 95 Junction (2024)

properties.description: Sighting by Jackson Nguyen, photograph.
properties.title: Ocala National Forest Evening Encounter (2024)

properties.description: Maria Rivera and her 12-year-old son were driving down Alligator Alley late at night. They saw a massive, humanoid creature covered in reddish-brown fur.
properties.title: Alligator Alley Roadside Sighting (2024)
